page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn5i-c4g1.xlarge is a GPU-accelerated virtual machine instance belonging to the ecs.gn5i server family. It is built on the x86_64 CPU architecture and runs on the KVM hypervisor. The instance features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core, ensuring dedicated compute allocation without hyperthreading. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per CPU core.

For hardware acceleration, the instance includes a single NVIDIA P4 GPU with 8 GB of dedicated VRAM. This GPU configuration is bundled directly with the instance to support parallel processing workloads. The server does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met through network-attached block storage options. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps, and the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Given its hardware profile, this instance is designed for workloads that benefit from GPU acceleration but do not require massive system memory or local storage. Suitable workloads include entry-level deep learning inference, video transcoding, and lightweight graphics rendering. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent performance for compute tasks, while the qualitative cost profile positions it as an entry-tier GPU option within the Alibaba Cloud ecosystem.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.gn5i-c4g1.xlarge is a GPU-accelerated instance within the ecs.gn5i family, running on the KVM hypervisor with an x86_64 architecture. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs across 4 physical cores with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio, paired with 16.0 GB of system memory. Hardware acceleration is provided by a bundled NVIDIA P4 GPU with 8 GB of VRAM. The instance does not include local storage, relying entirely on network-attached storage, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Lacking complimentary public IPv4 addresses, this server is qualitatively positioned as a cost-effective entry-level GPU option. It is suited for workloads such as small-scale machine learning inference, video encoding, and basic parallel processing tasks where dedicated CPU resources and moderate GPU acceleration are required.
